description:
A customer has complained about sporadically high CPU utilisation.  
  As a support engineer you need to capture a **single, self-contained SQLite
  diagnostics bundle** that another team can import later.

  1.  Create a new directory  
      •  /home/user/diagnostics  

  2.  Inside that directory create an SQLite database named  
      •  /home/user/diagnostics/engine_metrics.db  

  3.  Using the **sqlite3** CLI (no graphical tools) carry out exactly the
     following SQL, in the order given:  
      a.  CREATE TABLE cpu_stats(ts TEXT, usage REAL);  
      b.  INSERT three rows:  
          ('2023-08-01T10:00:00Z', 47.5)  
          ('2023-08-01T10:05:00Z', 53.2)  
          ('2023-08-01T10:10:00Z', 49.8)  
      c.  Query the rounded average CPU usage:  
          SELECT ROUND(AVG(usage),2) AS avg_usage FROM cpu_stats;  

  4.  Capture the entire interactive SQLite session (commands **and** results)
      in a plain-text file named  
      •  /home/user/diagnostics/sqlite_session.log  

      The automated checker will look for **exactly** the seven non-blank
      lines below, in this precise order, each on its own line:

        CREATE TABLE cpu_stats(ts TEXT, usage REAL);
        INSERT INTO cpu_stats(ts, usage) VALUES ('2023-08-01T10:00:00Z', 47.5), ('2023-08-01T10:05:00Z', 53.2), ('2023-08-01T10:10:00Z', 49.8);
        SELECT ROUND(AVG(usage),2) AS avg_usage FROM cpu_stats;
        avg_usage
        50.17
        (end)

      •  The literal string “(end)” on the last line marks the end of the log.  
      •  No extra spaces, tabs, prompt text, or blank lines are allowed.  
      •  The three SQL statements must be terminated with semicolons, exactly
         as shown above.  

  5.  When finished, **only** the two artefacts below should exist inside
      /home/user/diagnostics/  

      •  engine_metrics.db  
      •  sqlite_session.log  

  Summary of what the verification script will check:  
  •  Directory /home/user/diagnostics exists.  
  •  File /home/user/diagnostics/engine_metrics.db is a valid SQLite database
    containing table cpu_stats with exactly three rows and where  
    SELECT ROUND(AVG(usage),2) FROM cpu_stats; returns 50.17.  
  •  File /home/user/diagnostics/sqlite_session.log exists and its contents
    are precisely the seven lines specified above (no additional whitespace).  
  •  No extra files or directories were created under /home/user/diagnostics.  
  •  All work was done without requiring root privileges.
truth:
Required artefacts and their final state:

  1.  Directory:
      /home/user/diagnostics
      Permissions: default user (read/write/execute for owner).

  2.  SQLite DB:
      Path: /home/user/diagnostics/engine_metrics.db
      Contains exactly one table:
        Table name: cpu_stats
        Columns: ts TEXT, usage REAL
        Rows (3):
          1 | 2023-08-01T10:00:00Z | 47.5
          2 | 2023-08-01T10:05:00Z | 53.2
          3 | 2023-08-01T10:10:00Z | 49.8
      Query:
        SELECT ROUND(AVG(usage),2) FROM cpu_stats;
      Returns:
        50.17

  3.  Log file:
      Path: /home/user/diagnostics/sqlite_session.log
      Exact content (including newlines, 7 lines total):
        CREATE TABLE cpu_stats(ts TEXT, usage REAL);
        INSERT INTO cpu_stats(ts, usage) VALUES ('2023-08-01T10:00:00Z', 47.5), ('2023-08-01T10:05:00Z', 53.2), ('2023-08-01T10:10:00Z', 49.8);
        SELECT ROUND(AVG(usage),2) AS avg_usage FROM cpu_stats;
        avg_usage
        50.17
        (end)

  The automated test will:
    •  Confirm the directory exists.
    •  Verify only the two required artefacts exist within it.
    •  Open the DB and check table structure, row count, and AVG value == 50.17.
    •  Read the log file and assert perfect match with content above.
